headlines.title: Rutte attempts to ease Trump-NATO rift over Iran ahead of annual summit
headlines.snippet: NATO chief Mark Rutte visited the White House to discuss tensions with Trump ahead of an upcoming NATO summit. The meeting aimed to ease disagreements concerning Iran.

headlines.title: Russian opposition leader jailed over anti-war social media posts
headlines.snippet: A Russian opposition leader has been jailed for making anti-war social media posts. The sentencing comes amid ongoing crackdowns on dissent within Russia.

headlines.title: Venezuela earthquakes live: Tremors of 7.5, 7.2 collapse Caracas buildings
headlines.snippet: A magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck Venezuela near Moron, followed by a stronger 7.5 magnitude quake in the same region. Buildings in Caracas reportedly collapsed.

headlines.title: Buildings collapse in Venezuelan capital after powerful earthquakes hit
headlines.snippet: Buildings have collapsed in Venezuela's capital after powerful earthquakes struck the country. The tremors caused significant damage in Caracas.

headlines.title: Two years on, Kenyan families still seek justice after Gen Z protests
headlines.snippet: Two years after Gen Z protests, Kenyan families are still seeking justice for those killed and missing. They state that investigations have stalled and accountability remains elusive.

headlines.title: Trump criticises European allies over lack of support during war on Iran
headlines.snippet: Trump criticized European allies for a perceived lack of support during the war on Iran. Speaking with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, Trump stated that while US assistance wasn't needed, stronger backing would have been welcomed.

headlines.title: Trump casts doubt on US responsibility for deadly Iran school strike
headlines.snippet: Trump expressed doubt regarding US responsibility for a deadly strike on an Iranian primary school on February 28. He stated he had seen no evidence implicating the United States in the incident, which occurred during the US-Israeli war on Iran.

headlines.title: Vance says US reviewing conditions for Türkiye's F-35 purchase
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance is reviewing conditions for Turkey's F-35 purchase with the Pentagon. The review will determine if Turkey has met legal requirements for receiving the fighter jets, a process that involves congressional approval.

headlines.title: UN nuclear chief says inspections in Iran will go ahead despite Tehran's caution
headlines.snippet: The head of the IAEA stated that UN nuclear inspectors will visit Iran's enrichment sites. This will proceed despite Tehran's indication that inspections would only occur after a final agreement is reached.

headlines.title: Israeli army says it struck two people in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The Israeli military announced it struck two individuals in southern Lebanon. The army stated the individuals were in a vehicle and posed a threat to its forces in the occupied area.

headlines.title: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah killed in explosion in Yemen’s Mukalla
headlines.snippet: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah was killed in an explosion in Mukalla, Yemen. The circumstances of the explosion are under investigation.

headlines.title: Wright: 20 Million Barrels of oil Exited Hormuz in Last 24 Hours
headlines.snippet: Approximately 20 million barrels of oil exited the Strait of Hormuz within the last 24 hours, according to Wright. This figure highlights recent oil transit activity.

headlines.title: Trump Says Iran War ‘Going Very Well’
headlines.snippet: Trump stated that the war on Iran is 'going very well.' This assessment comes amidst ongoing military operations and diplomatic developments.

headlines.title: Federal judge bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote
headlines.snippet: A federal judge has issued an order preventing Trump from enforcing a requirement for proof of citizenship to vote. The ruling impacts election procedures.

headlines.title: 500 US Aircraft Took Off From Italy for Iran Strikes: NATO Chief
headlines.snippet: NATO Chief indicated that 500 US aircraft took off from Italy for strikes against Iran. This operation involved significant air support from NATO member Italy.

headlines.title: Trump seeks $88 bn in extra funding, mostly for Iran war
headlines.snippet: The White House has requested $87.6 billion in supplemental funding, with a substantial portion allocated for the war on Iran. The funds are primarily designated for the Pentagon.

headlines.title: Colombia president-elect to work with Israel 'like never before'
headlines.snippet: The president-elect of Colombia has stated a commitment to work with Israel unlike ever before. This signals a significant shift in diplomatic relations.

headlines.title: UK government's answer to stolen Palestinian land? Ad watchdog
headlines.snippet: The UK government's response to the sale of illegal Israeli settlement homes in London has been criticized as weak. An advertising watchdog is the focus of this criticism.

headlines.title: Syria trial opens for Assad cousin accused of war crimes
headlines.snippet: A trial has opened in Syria for a cousin of Assad accused of war crimes. The court is considering acts attributed to him as crimes against humanity and war crimes.

headlines.title: US to progress with Turkey jet engine sales ahead of NATO summit
headlines.snippet: The United States plans to advance Turkey jet engine sales prior to the NATO summit. This move occurs amidst ongoing diplomatic discussions between the two nations.

headlines.snippet: Activists from a Gaza flotilla recounted their detention by Israeli forces, describing the experience as 'inhumane.' Their testimonies highlight the conditions faced by those attempting to reach Gaza.

headlines.snippet: The Crown Prince met with CEOs of leading technology companies in Silicon Valley. The discussions likely focused on innovation and investment opportunities.

headlines.snippet: Iran has declared that a deal to end the Mideast war is a 'declaration of US defeat.' This statement reflects Iran's perspective on the recent diplomatic agreement.

headlines.snippet: Netanyahu stated that troops will not withdraw from Lebanon as long as he remains Prime Minister. This declaration comes after an Israeli drone strike killed two people near Nabatieh.

headlines.snippet: Washington talks have stalled on their second day, with disagreements centering on 'pilot zones.' The negotiations are aimed at resolving ongoing issues between the involved parties.

headlines.snippet: A UN Commission of Inquiry found that Israeli forces are deliberately targeting children in Gaza, committing genocide. The report details "unprecedented death, injury..." and calls for action.
